title: hemigraphis flava
intro: Hemigraphis flava, also known as gold-dust plant or Indian chalk, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to tropical Asia and commonly used in ornamental landscaping due to its attractive golden-green foliage. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Hemigraphis flava include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support skin health. Hemigraphis flava can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, and has also been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine as a paste or powder for topical applications.
